description_long: This object is a robust stone guard stone, featuring a prominent cross-shaped top mounted on a rectangular pedestal base. The surface is weathered, with visible signs of erosion and discoloration, suggesting long-term exposure to the elements. The base is square and solid, with some moss or lichen growth along the lower edges, indicating placement in an outdoor, possibly grassy environment. The cross section is thick and rounded, with the arms of the cross blending smoothly into the central shaft. The stone appears to be roughly hewn, with a matte, rough finish and irregular surface texture. There are no visible inscriptions or decorative engravings, giving it a utilitarian and historical appearance.
description: A weathered stone guard stone with a cross-shaped top on a square pedestal, showing signs of age and outdoor exposure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cross shape and the square pedestal base, with visible surface wear and moss at the bottom.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the cross and the depth of the pedestal, with rough stone texture visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cross and pedestal, with more pronounced discoloration and weathering.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profile of the cross and pedestal, with irregularities in the stone surface.
attribute_location: Typically found at the entrance to driveways, historical sites, or along the edge of urban streets to protect corners and prevent vehicle encroachment onto pedestrian areas.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright with the base resting on or slightly embedded in the ground for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Surface shows signs of chipping and erosion, especially on the edges and corners of the cross and pedestal. Discoloration and moss growth are present near the base, likely due to weathering and ground moisture. The top and exposed faces are smoother from rain and wind abrasion.
